commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=87f77170350b94fcd1fccedf71b2ef9bb6ef3026)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an image-runtime catalog input fallback plus regression tests for preserving explicit text-only model input overrides.

PR surface: Source +93, Tests +85. Total +178 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review finding: source inspection shows generated agent models.json uses top-level `providers`, while the PR reads `parsed.models`. I did not run tests because this review was read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor needs to repair the compatibility bug and add real behavior proof; this should not be routed to automated repair while the external PR proof gate is unmet.

target.title: fix(media-understanding): resolve image model input from catalog, preserve explicit text-only (#92104)
target.body:
## Summary

Fix the image/pdf tool failing with "Model does not support images" for models whose `input` field is omitted in `models.providers` or agent `models.json`. Preserve explicit `input` overrides so existing text-only configurations are not silently changed.

## Root Cause

In `resolveImageRuntime`, `resolveProviderModelInput` defaults to `["text"]` when the user omits `input` from their model config. The resolution chain prefers inline matches over the bundled catalog, so the resolved model lacks image capability — even though the catalog declares it as `["text","image"]`.

The v3 fix adds a catalog fallback gated by `hasExplicitModelInput`, which checks both `cfg.models.providers` and the agent-local `models.json` for an explicit `input` field. When no explicit input is declared, the bundled catalog's image capability is merged. When input IS explicit (any value, including `["text"]`), the user's choice is preserved unchanged.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: Models with omitted `input` → catalog fills image capability. Models with explicit `input` (cfg or models.json) → user choice preserved. Both paths verified by regression tests.

**Real environment tested**: Linux x64 (RHEL 8), Node.js v24.13.1, pnpm v11.2.2, openclaw at 79d7defd0b (origin/main)

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```
cd /home/0668001085/workspace/openclaw
pnpm test -- --run src/media-understanding/image.test.ts
```

**Evidence after fix** — terminal output:
```
 RUN  v4.1.7 /home/0668001085/workspace/openclaw

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  28 passed (28)    ← includes 2 new regression tests
   Start at  20:56:59
   Duration  11.07s (transform 3.11s, setup 453ms, import 5.24s, tests 5.03s)

[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 20.56s
```

Regression tests added:
```
✓ respects explicit input: ['text'] in models.providers (#92104)
✓ respects explicit input: ['text'] in agent models.json (#92104)
```

**Observed result after fix**:
- `input` omitted → catalog supplies `["text","image"]` → tool proceeds
- `input: ["text"]` in `cfg.models.providers` → catalog skipped, tool rejects
- `input: ["text"]` in agent `models.json` → catalog skipped, tool rejects
- Zero regressions (28/28 tests pass)

**What was not tested**: End-to-end gateway with live API keys (requires real credentials).

## Regression Test Plan

- [x] `pnpm test -- --run src/media-understanding/image.test.ts` — 28/28
- [x] `models.providers` explicit input preserved
- [x] Agent `models.json` explicit input preserved
- [ ] Change is minimal (2 files, +178 lines, additive fallback)

---
*AI-assisted: built with Claude Code*
target.author: zhangqueping
